Patch Set 1:

This is a good catch. I have a few questions:

--- a/lustre/ptlrpc/client.c
+++ b/lustre/ptlrpc/client.c
@@ -1673,12 +1673,21 @@ int ptlrpc_check_set(const struct lu_env *env, struct ptlrpc_request_set *set)
                         * sure that all rdma transfers finished and will
                         * not corrupt any data.
                         */
+#if 0
                         if (ptlrpc_client_recv_or_unlink(req) ||
                             ptlrpc_client_bulk_active(req))
                                 continue;
+#else
+                       if (ptlrpc_client_recv_or_unlink(req))
+                               continue;
+
+                       ptlrpc_unregister_bulk(req, 1);
+                       if (ptlrpc_client_bulk_active(req))
+                               continue;
+          
+#endif

Isn't the above patch good enough to fix this problem? RQ_PHASE_UNREG_RPC and RQ_PHASE_UNREG_BULK are basically the same thing, maybe not necessary to have two states.

2. The test 115b keeps failing on my test node:
== recovery-small test 115b: write: late REQ MDunlink and no bulk ==================================== 18:14:54 (1447974894)
Filesystem                  1K-blocks  Used Available Use% Mounted on
192.168.175.129@tcp:/lustre    717104 26972    633688   5% /mnt/lustre
1+0 records in
1+0 records out
4096 bytes (4.1 kB) copied, 2.0108 s, 2.0 kB/s
 recovery-small test_115b: @@@@@@ FAIL: dd success 
  Trace dump:
  = ./../tests/test-framework.sh:4812:error_noexit()
  = ./../tests/test-framework.sh:4843:error()
  = recovery-small.sh:2160:test_115_write()
  = recovery-small.sh:2174:test_115b()
  = ./../tests/test-framework.sh:5090:run_one()
  = ./../tests/test-framework.sh:5127:run_one_logged()
  = ./../tests/test-framework.sh:4950:run_test()
  = recovery-small.sh:2176:main()
Dumping lctl log to /tmp/test_logs/1447974891/recovery-small.test_115b.*.1447974896.log
Dumping logs only on local client.
FAIL 115b (2s)

I ran it on my vm. Can you see the same problem?

Patch Set 1:

the above excerpt is wrong because you do not always need to unlink bulk when reply is unlinked - i.e. having a reply, you are waiting for a bulk, after unlinking its buffers, you will not get it.

Patch Set 1:

but it checks and waits for bulk to become inactive.

Since the server starts bulk transfer before replying, there should be no logic issues. I meant your patch is good to fix this problem but it seems unnecessary to maintain two identical states.

Do you have a reproducer for this issue on real hardware? Maybe we can just give it a try.

Patch Set 1:

no, it does it now what is wrong, because it should wait just for 1 thing in each particular time. having 1 state is not enough to distinguish them - this is the logic issue. 

no, i cannot fail it in LNET on router, but I gave you an emulator (the part of the patch which is the test only without the fix) which is enough to understand the logic from the lustre logs.

parents.subject: LU-7323 test: Unknown symbol mb_cache_create (err 0)" on RHEL7

commit.subject: LU-7434 ptlrpc: lost bulk leads to a hang
commit.message:
LU-7434 ptlrpc: lost bulk leads to a hang

The reverse order of request_out_callback() and reply_in_callback()
puts the RPC into UNREGISTERING state, which is waiting for RPC &
bulk md unlink, whereas only RPC md unlink has been called so far.
If bulk is lost, even expired_set does not check for UNREGISTERING
state.

The same for write if server returns an error.

This phase is ambiguous, split to UNREG_RPC and UNREG_BULK.
